title: AEAD MAC leaked in error message and timing attack vulnerability
problem: In `aes_decrypt`, the calculated AEAD tag is compared using the non-constant time `!=` operator, enabling timing attacks (CWE-208). Furthermore, if the tags do not match, the application leaks the valid `calculated_tag` in the error message string, allowing an attacker to trivially forge the MAC for any modified ciphertext by simply reading the error message (CWE-209).

title: Memory leak of ECC context on sign failure
problem: In `ecdsa_sign`, if `pkey_sign` fails (`ret != 0`), the function immediately returns an error without freeing the allocated `key_ctx`. This causes a memory leak of the unmanaged C ECC context.

title: Potential Use-After-Free due to dropped local vectors
problem: In `key_to_ctx`, `pub_key` and `prv_key` are copied into local vectors (`pub_buf` and `prv_buf`), and their data pointers are set into the ECC context via `ecc_set_pub`/`ecc_set_prv`. However, these vectors are dropped at the end of their respective `if` scopes. If the C library retains these pointers, the returned `ctx` will contain dangling pointers, causing a Use-After-Free. Bypassing `.to_vec()` and directly using the caller's slice pointers is both safer (since the caller's slices live longer) and more efficient.

title: Compilation error due to missing unsafe block for union field access
problem: In Rust, reading or modifying sub-fields of a union field directly (e.g., `self.key.ecc_pub.data = ...`) is considered an unsafe operation and requires an `unsafe` block. Without it, this code will fail to compile. Assigning the entire variant struct at once avoids the need for `unsafe`.

title: Compilation error due to missing unsafe block for union field access
problem: Modifying sub-fields of a union field directly requires an `unsafe` block in Rust. Assigning the entire variant struct at once resolves the compilation error securely.

title: Missing null pointer check for context allocation
problem: `sha2_256_newctx()` can return a null pointer if the internal allocation fails. Passing a null pointer directly to `sha2_256_init()` and storing it in the `Sha256` struct without checking will lead to undefined behavior or segmentation faults in C.

title: Unnecessary consumption of private key
problem: `ecdsa_sign` takes `prv_key` as `Vec<u8>` instead of `&[u8]`. This unnecessarily consumes the private key, forcing the caller to clone it if they want to reuse the key for multiple signatures.

title: Unnecessary consumption of public key
problem: `ecdsa_verify` takes `pub_key` as `Vec<u8>` instead of `&[u8]`. This forces the caller to transfer ownership or clone the public key, resulting in suboptimal performance and ergonomics.
